concerns.text: FR-006 mandates a 'Linear Mixed-Effects Model (LMM)', but the tasks (T028, T026a) implement a 'Generalized Linear Mixed Model (GLMM)'. Task T026a attempts to update the spec, but the spec itself (FR-006) remains unchanged in the current artifact. There is no task covering the implementation of the LMM as currently specified, and the GLMM implementation lacks a corresponding FR in the spec. This creates a gap where the spec requirement (LMM) is unimplemented and the implemented requirement (GLMM) is un-specified.

SC-002 requires a 'non-inferiority margin of a small, predefined absolute percentage point threshold'. While tasks T028, T032, and T042 implement the logic, no task explicitly defines or sources the specific numerical value for this 'predefined' margin. The spec leaves the value undefined ('small, predefined'), and the tasks assume it exists or will be hardcoded without a task to define it in the data model or config. This leaves the success criterion untestable as written.

FR-002 requires generating a pool of tasks reused across 'all 50 profiles'. Task T006 generates 'a few profiles' per domain, and T014b targets a scale of '10 profiles'. There is no task explicitly covering the generation of the full set of 50 profiles required by the spec's FR-002 and US-3 theoretical matrix, nor is there a task to generate the full 200 tasks per profile mentioned in US-3. The tasks only cover the 'feasible subset' (10 profiles, 50 tasks), leaving the full spec requirement unimplemented.

Tasks T038, T039, T040, T041, and T042 in Phase 7 introduce new validation logic (strict failure on simulated profiles, pre-flight OOM checks, effective sample size validation) that are not explicitly tagged to any FR/SC in the spec. While they address reviewer concerns, they lack the `[FR-NNN]` or `[SC-NNN]` tags required to prove coverage of the spec's requirements. These tasks appear to be reactive additions without a corresponding spec requirement, creating a 'stale tag' or 'orphan task' situation where the task exists but the spec requirement it supposedly covers is missing or implicit.
